lines.text: Pardon my intrusion. Allow me to introduce myself. I am Cristoforo, a modest playwright visiting Ragunna in search of inspiration for my latest work.
A pleasure to make your acquaintance. I'm working on a play—tales of pilgrims striving to reach the Sentinel's promised land...
Word of a new Pilgrim's Sail about to depart brought me here. I came hoping to glean fresh ideas.
Yes. When Ragunnesi first settled here, they revered the Sentinel. The Carnevale was their tribute—a sacred rite to commune with it.
It seemed the Sentinel Imperator preferred not to flaunt its power overtly to command the people's awe and respect.
Yet, amidst the Carnevale's revelry, it performed miracles, keeping the people aligned with its guidance.
Legend has it that, moved by its grace, the most devout believer braved storms and mists to find the Sentinel—thus was born the tradition of the Pilgrim's Sail.
Should you wish, I have more stories to tell.
Phrolova, it seems we walk different paths. I don't share your fondness for that sort of... puppet theater.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: I merely place the characters on their stage and they act out their fates, driven by their own volition.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: To remain courageous amidst absurdity, to pursue a spark of divine grace—this, to me, is the truest essence of humanity, don't you think?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Even the most pristine cadence of death pales beside the untamed, fervent, and tenacious spirits of the living.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: In their journey, they resolutely overcome all obstacles: they pursue, they tear, they burn—simply because they desire to.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: I think you should leave your secluded mansion. Take a walk and listen to the voices scattered across the world.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: I am certain that, even without any direction, {Male=he;Female=she} will walk the path that destiny has laid before {Male=him;Female=her}.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: All things ascend towards a singular point of convergence, and what awaits at the end is... immortality.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Before the tides of fate, we are all but…
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: …humble servants.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: No, Madam. I wouldn't take all the credit.
The architects of this tragedy were the Septimontians themselves. A collective murder so entertainingly woven. With blood on every hand, who's left to point a finger?
But I must admit, I'm astonished you would endure all this for strangers and lands you've never set foot on.
Heh... Madam, surely you know you alone cannot undo my story.
Let's just say it was inspired by the Somnoire. Imagination, after all, remains humanity's sharpest blade.
Of course. And through Leviathan's power, I was able to "read" that frequency and "depict" you back into reality.
Leviathan's power of assimilation blurs the line between imagination and existence, turning fiction into fact.
But of course, none of it would have worked until Leviathan slipped the Sentinel's leash and reclaimed the reins of the Dark Tide.
A trial, nothing less.
Civilization's infancy must come to an end. So long as humanity remains cradled in the Sentinel's arms, it will never walk, let alone evolve. That is why I wrote this final act.
And those who cannot transcend their limits and overcome their lesser natures, who remain imprisoned by their own making... Well, such souls deserve no salvation.
You sound disappointed in how this story ends.
It has already been written into the script.
But until I reach it, as long as breath remains in me, I will haunt the stage like a phantom. A reminder... that one must always look forward, sharpen thy self, and temper thy soul.
Insanity, reason… or something in between... All are choices born of free will. And only through free will can we defy the surging tides of fate.
That is why we both seek to change it. For beneath such tides... no one is content to remain merely a humble servant.
Forgive me. But haven't we already seized what we truly desired?
You see, even <ano=Journeying Paradise>the Beyond forged from Leviathan's power</ano> couldn't endure. It fell short of the perfection you long for.
But if you wish to try again, I remain at your service.
Not much, but enough to make use of.
As you wish. My sincerest gratitude for your wonderful performance, Lady Phrolova. May you find the strength to create the Beyond you once envisioned.
Of all of us, you are the last one with any right to judge her for following her whims.
Friends, it's been too long. I gather you were also listening in on that conversation about the Golden Anthem's last work?
"Resentful of the Order's verdict, they committed to paper a curse upon Rinascita and Ragunna."
"Those favored by the Divinity were the most willing to raise the blade of rebellion, for they alone knew how to make Divinity bleed and doctrines crumble."
"Though they died in exile to a great fire, their resentment festered in their music and poetry, transforming into horrors known as Daemons."
"Like the fire that dragged them to hell, these Daemons will spread throughout the land and burn all of Ragunna."
This article was recently published in the Ragunna Literary Journal... Quite the perspective, wouldn't you say? Vengeance borne of resentment—such dark and twisted characters are hard to resist.
Perhaps someone unwittingly played that tune just now and opened the Plushies to possession by the Daemons.
Then again, it's all conjecture. Those in the Poets Society died years ago, and who's to say what really happened?
Now, if those vengeful spirits really do exist... well, out of all the patrons here, they went straight for you, {PlayerName}. Curious and curiouser...
By the way, it just so happens that I made a copy of the Poet Society's last work.
Here, take it. Consider it a little souvenir from your... spirited encounter. Maybe it'll prove useful later.
Goodbye friends, a change of venue is in order. I must finish my play.
My Lord Primus, it seems your plan has not gone quite as you'd hoped.
You sought to revive the blasphemous Poets Society, spreading their legacy to stoke the fires of prejudice. And through my aid, summon Daemons to sow fear across Ragunna City...
Ah! Truly the words of one in possession of great power! Such lines are rare even in plays.
And yet... why do you seem so restless, Lord Fenrico? This is hardly the character I expected from you.
Is it that foreign Laureate that unsettles you so? Or perhaps that insignificant Toccata girl?
Or is it... something else you seek?
Of course. As you will, Lord Fenrico.
As you've said yourself, out of respect for our partnership, some things... are better left unsaid.
There was no deception. Indeed, I was seeking inspiration. In the end... I found a story about birds in a cage.
The birds, having uncovered the truth, must decide—will they choose to fly into the unknown, or remain caged within the familiar?
Ah, Lord Fenrico, a word of reminder...
Just as the young Toccata mentioned, how long has it been since you last dreamed of your true "Divinity"?
Armed with a wooden spear and a broken helmet, the knight charged at the windmill...
His squire, ever loyal, trailed close behind as if becoming one with the knight. Together they charged again and again, true knights in spirit, ignoring the countless wounds they bore.
Truly astonishing... Even if you took such a script, what could you possibly accomplish, "Hecate"?
The young birds left their cage, flapping frail wings as they fell to the ground.
Coming here implies you worry about tomorrow's outcome.
You intend to reveal the truth of the Sentinel to the public. But that revelation... it comes with a price. One you've paid before, as a child. Remember?
The truth has its thorns, and the Golden Anthem has already paid its price. Do you wish to relive their fate?
Ah, so you've already tasted their fate, haven't you? You and your family, banished in the name of... blasphemy!
Such a dramatic turn of events! As cantors of the Sentinel, both the Toccata family and the Poets Society committed the same crime. Tell me, what is the connection between you and those poets?
Opportunity. Indeed... The moment of choice dawns once more.
Do you intend to repeat history? This time, dragging {PlayerName} along with you? Boarding the Pilgrim's Sail and clinging to hope as the storm carries you away.
Isn't this what you're afraid of?
You've unraveled the hand behind it all... but the Order will never allow such truths to see the light.
{PlayerName}, Ms. Carlotta, and all those you've gathered along the way will be forced to bear the consequences of your quest for the truth.
And perhaps even that... "Blessed Maiden" whose whereabouts remain a mystery to this day.
Even so, will you two still insist on stepping onto that stage tomorrow?
Have you really made your choice? I look forward to the arrival of tomorrow, Miss Poet.
A fascinating story indeed! You've certainly piqued everyone's curiosity.
This speech you made just now... You're implying the poets of the Golden Anthem were framed and their works deliberately misinterpreted?
If memory serves, official records of the Order of the Deep state the poets were charged with blasphemy against the Sentinel.
Surely you're not suggesting this was all the Order's doing? I dare say... such remarks would be considered a grave crime punishable by banishment.
Haha, I'm afraid you misunderstand... I admit I do get that question quite often. I am but a traveling playwright.
Your presentation brings me back to a play I wrote in my youth.
A group of birds locked inside a cage. Convinced they will never fly, they do nothing but stand on a perch.
Then one day, the cage door opened. The young birds flapped their fragile wings attempting to fly, but plummeted to the ground one by one.
Many consider their fate a tragic one. What say you?
And what of those who chose to remain in the cage, having witnessed their predecessors fall to their demise?
Will they still dare to reach for the skies after realizing the cost of choosing to fly? Does the "truth" exist to protect, or to harm? That is the question.
Miss Poet... I look forward to tomorrow's arrival.
What a beautiful ending, my near-perfect protagonist. I must confess... even I didn't see this coming.
Should this be your final act? No. No, allow me this small indulgence, as your creator.
The quill of fate may be sharp and cold, but still, I want to see how far the soul I created can go.
After all, it's the stories that sink deepest into the mire that stir the heart most, don't you think?
